pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 21 (JOB_DIR definition), line 30 (job restoration message), export_result.sh line 14 (JOB_NAME variable), and setup_task.sh line 38 (job description)

Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 49-55 (shell command exits with 'exit 0'), export_result.sh line 41 (checks RESULT=='SUCCESS'), verifier.py line 42 (validates build_success flag), task.json description line 5 ('ensure it completes successfully')

Code analysis: task.json description line 5 states 'WITHOUT restarting the Jenkins service/container', setup_task.sh lines 15-16 (records initial PID for anti-gaming), export_result.sh lines 54-62 (PID change detection logic), export_result.sh lines 64-74 (container uptime check), verifier.py lines 70-79 (penalizes restart by removing 10-point bonus)

privileged_info_summary: The task 'register_restored_job@1' involves making a manually restored Jenkins job named 'Legacy-Payroll' visible to Jenkins without restarting the service. The job is configured to execute a shell script that exits with code 0 (SUCCESS). The implementation uses PID tracking and container uptime checks to detect and penalize any service restart. All data is synthetic and generated at runtime by the setup_task.sh script, which creates the job configuration as a freestyle Jenkins job with a simple shell builder command.
